an9.13:1.1.pli: Atha kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sāriputtena saddhiṁ sammodi.
an9.13:1.1.eng: Then Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita went up to Venerable Sāriputta, and exchanged greetings with him.

an9.13:1.2.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
an9.13:1.2.eng: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to Sāriputta:

an9.13:1.3.pli: “kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ, taṁ me kammaṁ samparāyavedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:1.3.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds to be experienced in this life be experienced by me in lives to come’?”

an9.13:2.1.pli: “Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ samparāyavedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:2.1.eng: “Then is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds to be experienced in lives to come be experienced by me in this life’?”

an9.13:3.1.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ sukhavedanīyaṁ, taṁ me kammaṁ dukkhavedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:3.1.eng: “Is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds to be experienced as pleasant be experienced by me as painful’?”

an9.13:4.1.pli: “Kiṁ panāvuso, sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ dukkhavedanīyaṁ, taṁ me kammaṁ sukhavedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:4.1.eng: “Then is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds to be experienced as painful be experienced by me as pleasant’?”

an9.13:5.1.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ paripakkavedanīyaṁ, taṁ me kammaṁ aparipakkavedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:5.1.eng: “Is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds to be experienced when ripe be experienced by me when unripe’?”

an9.13:6.1.pli: “Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ aparipakkavedanīyaṁ, taṁ me kammaṁ paripakkavedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:6.1.eng: “Then is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds to be experienced when unripe be experienced by me when ripe’?”

an9.13:7.1.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ bahuvedanīyaṁ, taṁ me kammaṁ appavedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:7.1.eng: “Is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds to be experienced a lot be experienced by me a little’?”

an9.13:8.1.pli: “Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ appavedanīyaṁ, taṁ me kammaṁ bahuvedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:8.1.eng: “Then is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds to be experienced a little be experienced by me a lot’?”

an9.13:9.1.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ vedanīyaṁ, taṁ me kammaṁ avedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:9.1.eng: “Is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds to be experienced by me be not experienced’?”

an9.13:10.1.pli: “Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, ‘yaṁ kammaṁ avedanīyaṁ, taṁ me kammaṁ vedanīyaṁ hotū’ti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:10.1.eng: “Then is the spiritual life lived under the Buddha for this purpose: ‘May deeds not to be experienced be experienced’?”

an9.13:11.1.pli: “‘Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ samparāyavedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:11.1.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, when you were asked whether the spiritual life was lived under the Buddha so that deeds to be experienced in this life are experienced in lives to come, you said, ‘Certainly not’.

an9.13:12.1.pli: ‘Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ samparāyavedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:12.1.eng: When you were asked whether the spiritual life was lived under the Buddha so that deeds to be experienced in lives to come are experienced in this life …

an9.13:13.1.pli: ‘Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ sukhavedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ dukkhavedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:13.1.eng: deeds to be experienced as pleasant are experienced as painful …

an9.13:14.1.pli: ‘Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ dukkhavedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ sukhavedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:14.1.eng: deeds to be experienced as painful are experienced as pleasant …

an9.13:15.1.pli: ‘Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ paripakkavedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ aparipakkavedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:15.1.eng: deeds to be experienced when ripe are experienced when unripe …

an9.13:16.1.pli: ‘Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ aparipakkavedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ paripakkavedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:16.1.eng: deeds to be experienced when unripe are experienced when ripe …

an9.13:17.1.pli: ‘Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ bahuvedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ appavedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:17.1.eng: deeds to be experienced a lot are experienced a little …

an9.13:18.1.pli: ‘Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ appavedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ bahuvedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:18.1.eng: deeds to be experienced a little are experienced a lot …

an9.13:19.1.pli: ‘Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ vedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ avedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:19.1.eng: deeds to be experienced are not experienced …

an9.13:20.1.pli: ‘Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, yaṁ kammaṁ avedanīyaṁ taṁ me kammaṁ vedanīyaṁ hotūti, etassa atthāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘no hidaṁ, āvuso’ti vadesi.
an9.13:20.1.eng: When you were asked whether the spiritual life was lived under the Buddha so that deeds not to be experienced are experienced, you said, ‘Certainly not.’

an9.13:20.2.pli: Atha kimatthaṁ carahāvuso, bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti?
an9.13:20.2.eng: Then what exactly is the purpose of leading the spiritual life under the Buddha?”

an9.13:21.1.pli: “Yaṁ khvassa, āvuso, aññātaṁ adiṭṭhaṁ appattaṁ asacchikataṁ anabhisametaṁ, tassa ñāṇāya dassanāya pattiyā sacchikiriyāya abhisamayāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatīti.
an9.13:21.1.eng: “Reverend, the spiritual life is lived under the Buddha to know, see, attain, realize, and comprehend that which is unknown, unseen, unattained, unrealized, and uncomprehended.”

an9.13:21.2.pli: Kiṁ panassāvuso, aññātaṁ adiṭṭhaṁ appattaṁ asacchikataṁ anabhisametaṁ, yassa ñāṇāya dassanāya pattiyā sacchikiriyāya abhisamayāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatīti?
an9.13:21.2.eng: “But what is the unknown, unseen, unattained, unrealized, and uncomprehended?”

an9.13:21.3.pli: ‘Idaṁ dukkhan’ti khvassa, āvuso, aññātaṁ adiṭṭhaṁ appattaṁ asacchikataṁ anabhisametaṁ.

an9.13:21.4.pli: Tassa ñāṇāya dassanāya pattiyā sacchikiriyāya abhisamayāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussati.

an9.13:21.5.pli: ‘Ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti khvassa, āvuso …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho’ti khvassa, āvuso …pe…
an9.13:21.5.eng: ‘This is the origin of suffering.’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering.’ …

an9.13:21.6.pli: ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti khvassa, āvuso, aññātaṁ adiṭṭhaṁ appattaṁ asacchikataṁ anabhisametaṁ.
an9.13:21.6.eng: ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.’ …

an9.13:21.7.pli: Tassa ñāṇāya dassanāya pattiyā sacchikiriyāya abhisamayāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussati.

an9.13:21.8.pli: Idaṁ khvassa, āvuso, aññātaṁ adiṭṭhaṁ appattaṁ asacchikataṁ anabhisametaṁ.
an9.13:21.8.eng: This is the unknown, unseen, unattained, unrealized, and uncomprehended.

an9.13:21.9.pli: Tassa ñāṇāya dassanāya pattiyā sacchikiriyāya abhisamayāya bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī”ti.
an9.13:21.9.eng: The spiritual life is lived under the Buddha to know, see, attain, realize, and comprehend this.”

an9.14:1.1.pli: Atha kho āyasmā samiddhi yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ samiddhiṁ āyasmā sāriputto etadavoca:
an9.14:1.1.eng: Then Venerable Samiddhi went up to Venerable Sāriputta, bowed, and sat to one side. Venerable Sāriputta said to him:

an9.14:2.1.eng: “Samiddhi, based on what do thoughts arise in a person?”

an9.14:11.1.pli: “‘Kimārammaṇā, samiddhi, purisassa saṅkappavitakkā uppajjantī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘nāmarūpārammaṇā, bhante’ti vadesi.
an9.14:11.1.eng: “Samiddhi, when you were asked what is the basis on which thoughts arise in a person, you answered ‘name and form’.

an9.14:11.2.pli: ‘Te pana, samiddhi, kva nānattaṁ gacchantī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘dhātūsu, bhante’ti vadesi.

an9.14:11.3.pli: ‘Te pana, samiddhi, kiṁsamudayā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘phassasamudayā, bhante’ti vadesi.

an9.14:11.4.pli: ‘Te pana, samiddhi, kiṁsamosaraṇā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘vedanāsamosaraṇā, bhante’ti vadesi.

an9.14:11.5.pli: ‘Te pana, samiddhi, kiṁpamukhā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘samādhippamukhā, bhante’ti vadesi.

an9.14:11.6.pli: ‘Te pana, samiddhi, kiṁadhipateyyā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘satādhipateyyā, bhante’ti vadesi.

an9.14:11.7.pli: ‘Te pana, samiddhi, kiṁuttarā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘paññuttarā, bhante’ti vadesi.

an9.14:11.8.pli: ‘Te pana, samiddhi, kiṁsārā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘vimuttisārā, bhante’ti vadesi.

an9.14:11.9.pli: ‘Te pana, samiddhi, kiṁogadhā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘amatogadhā, bhante’ti vadesi.
an9.14:11.9.eng: what is their objective, you answered ‘freedom from death’.

an9.14:11.11.pli: Sādhu kho tvaṁ, samiddhi, puṭṭho puṭṭho vissajjesi, tena ca mā maññī”ti.
an9.14:11.11.eng: It’s good that you answered each question. But don’t get conceited because of that.”

an9.15:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, suppose there was a boil that was many years old.

an9.15:1.2.pli: Tassassu gaṇḍassa nava vaṇamukhāni nava abhedanamukhāni.
an9.15:1.2.eng: And that boil had nine orifices that were continually open wounds.

an9.15:1.4.pli: asuciyeva pagghareyya, duggandhaṁyeva pagghareyya, jegucchiyaṁyeva pagghareyya;
an9.15:1.4.eng: would be filthy, stinking, and disgusting.

an9.15:1.6.pli: asuciyeva pasaveyya, duggandhaṁyeva pasaveyya, jegucchiyaṁyeva pasaveyya.
an9.15:1.6.eng: would be filthy, stinking, and disgusting.

an9.15:2.1.pli: Gaṇḍoti kho, bhikkhave, imassetaṁ cātumahābhūtikassa kāyassa adhivacanaṁ mātāpettikasambhavassa odanakummāsūpacayassa aniccucchādanaparimaddanabhedanaviddhaṁsanadhammassa.
an9.15:2.1.eng: ‘Boil’ is a term for this body made up of the four principal states, produced by mother and father, built up from rice and porridge, liable to impermanence, to wearing away and erosion, to breaking up and destruction.

an9.15:2.2.pli: Tassassu gaṇḍassa nava vaṇamukhāni nava abhedanamukhāni.
an9.15:2.2.eng: And that boil has nine orifices that are continually open wounds.

an9.15:2.4.pli: asuciyeva paggharati, duggandhaṁyeva paggharati, jegucchiyaṁyeva paggharati;

an9.15:2.6.pli: asuciyeva pasavati, duggandhaṁyeva pasavati, jegucchiyaṁyeva pasavati.

an9.15:2.7.eng: So, mendicants, have no illusion about this body.”

an9.16:1.1.pli: “Navayimā, bhikkhave, saññā bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā honti mahānisaṁsā amatogadhā amatapariyosānā.
an9.16:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, these nine perceptions, when developed and cultivated, are very fruitful and beneficial. Their objective and culmination is freedom from death.

an9.16:1.3.pli: Asubhasaññā, maraṇasaññā, āhāre paṭikūlasaññā, sabbaloke anabhiratasaññā, aniccasaññā, anicce dukkhasaññā, dukkhe anattasaññā, pahānasaññā, virāgasaññā—
an9.16:1.3.eng: The perceptions of ugliness, death, repulsiveness of food, dissatisfaction with the whole world, impermanence, suffering in impermanence, not-self in suffering, giving up, and fading away.

an9.16:1.4.pli: imā kho, bhikkhave, nava saññā, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā honti mahānisaṁsā amatogadhā amatapariyosānā”ti.
an9.16:1.4.eng: These nine perceptions, when developed and cultivated, are very fruitful and beneficial. Their objective and culmination is freedom from death.”

an9.17:1.1.pli: “Navahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgataṁ kulaṁ anupagantvā vā nālaṁ upagantuṁ, upagantvā vā nālaṁ nisīdituṁ.
an9.17:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, visiting a family with nine factors is not worthwhile, or if you’ve already arrived, sitting down is not worthwhile.

an9.17:1.3.pli: Na manāpena paccuṭṭhenti, na manāpena abhivādenti, na manāpena āsanaṁ denti, santamassa pariguhanti, bahukampi thokaṁ denti, paṇītampi lūkhaṁ denti, asakkaccaṁ denti no sakkaccaṁ, na upanisīdanti dhammassavanāya, bhāsitamassa na sussūsanti.
an9.17:1.3.eng: They don’t politely rise, bow, or offer a seat. They hide what they have. Even when they have much they give little. Even when they have fine things they give coarse things. They give carelessly, not carefully. They don’t sit nearby to listen to the teachings. When you’re speaking, they don’t listen well.

an9.17:1.4.pli: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, navahaṅgehi samannāgataṁ kulaṁ anupagantvā vā nālaṁ upagantuṁ upagantvā vā nālaṁ nisīdituṁ.
an9.17:1.4.eng: Visiting a family with these nine factors is not worthwhile, or if you’ve already arrived, sitting down is not worthwhile.

an9.17:2.1.pli: Navahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgataṁ kulaṁ anupagantvā vā alaṁ upagantuṁ, upagantvā vā alaṁ nisīdituṁ.
an9.17:2.1.eng: Visiting a family with nine factors is worthwhile, or if you’ve already arrived, sitting down is worthwhile.

an9.17:2.3.pli: Manāpena paccuṭṭhenti, manāpena abhivādenti, manāpena āsanaṁ denti, santamassa na pariguhanti, bahukampi bahukaṁ denti, paṇītampi paṇītaṁ denti, sakkaccaṁ denti no asakkaccaṁ, upanisīdanti dhammassavanāya, bhāsitamassa sussūsanti.
an9.17:2.3.eng: They politely rise, bow, and offer a seat. They don’t hide what they have. When they have much they give much. When they have refined things they give refined things. They give carefully, not carelessly. They sit nearby to listen to the teachings. When you’re speaking, they listen well.

an9.17:2.4.pli: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, navahaṅgehi samannāgataṁ kulaṁ anupagantvā vā alaṁ upagantuṁ, upagantvā vā alaṁ nisīditun”ti.
an9.17:2.4.eng: Visiting a family with these nine factors is worthwhile, or if you’ve already arrived, sitting down is worthwhile.”

an9.18:1.1.pli: “Navahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgato uposatho upavuttho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro.
an9.18:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, the observance of the sabbath with its nine factors is very fruitful and beneficial and dazzling and bountiful.

an9.18:1.2.pli: Kathaṁ upavuttho ca, bhikkhave, navahaṅgehi samannāgato uposatho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro?
an9.18:1.2.eng: And how should it be observed?

an9.18:1.3.eng: It’s when a noble disciple reflects:

an9.18:1.4.pli: ‘yāvajīvaṁ arahanto pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭiviratā nihitadaṇḍā nihitasatthā lajjī dayāpannā sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampino viharanti;
an9.18:1.4.eng: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up killing living creatures, renouncing the rod and the sword. They are scrupulous and kind, and live full of sympathy for all living beings.

an9.18:1.5.pli: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭivirato nihitadaṇḍo nihitasattho lajjī dayāpanno sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampī viharāmi.
an9.18:1.5.eng: I, too, for this day and night will give up killing living creatures, renouncing the rod and the sword. I’ll be scrupulous and kind, and live full of sympathy for all living beings.

an9.18:1.6.pli: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi; uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
an9.18:1.6.eng: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’

an9.18:2.1.pli: ‘Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto uccāsayanamahāsayanaṁ pahāya uccāsayanamahāsayanā paṭiviratā nīcaseyyaṁ kappenti—mañcake vā tiṇasanthārake vā;
an9.18:2.1.eng: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up high and luxurious beds. They sleep in a low place, either a cot or a straw mat.

an9.18:2.2.pli: ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ uccāsayanamahāsayanaṁ pahāya uccāsayanamahāsayanā paṭivirato nīcaseyyaṁ kappemi—mañcake vā tiṇasanthārake vā.
an9.18:2.2.eng: I, too, for this day and night will give up high and luxurious beds. I’ll sleep in a low place, either a cot or a straw mat.

an9.18:2.3.pli: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi; uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
an9.18:2.3.eng: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’

an9.18:3.1.pli: Mettāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati, tathā dutiyaṁ tathā tatiyaṁ tathā catutthaṁ. Iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ mettāsahagatena cetasā vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjena pharitvā viharati.
an9.18:3.1.eng: They meditate spreading a heart full of love to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of love to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.

an9.18:4.1.pli: Evaṁ upavuttho kho, bhikkhave, navahaṅgehi samannāgato uposatho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro”ti.
an9.18:4.1.eng: The observance of the sabbath with its nine factors in this way is very fruitful and beneficial and dazzling and bountiful.”

an9.19:1.1.pli: “Imañca, bhikkhave, rattiṁ sambahulā devatā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho, bhikkhave, tā devatā maṁ etadavocuṁ:
an9.19:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, tonight, several glorious deities, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, came to me, bowed, stood to one side, and said to me:

an9.19:1.2.pli: ‘upasaṅkamiṁsu no, bhante, pubbe manussabhūtānaṁ pabbajitā agārāni.
an9.19:1.2.eng: ‘Sir, formerly when we were human beings, renunciates came to our homes.

an9.19:1.3.pli: Te mayaṁ, bhante, paccuṭṭhimha, no ca kho abhivādimha.
an9.19:1.3.eng: We politely rose for them, but we didn’t bow.

an9.19:1.4.pli: Tā mayaṁ, bhante, aparipuṇṇakammantā vippaṭisāriniyo paccānutāpiniyo hīnaṁ kāyaṁ upapannā’ti.
an9.19:1.4.eng: And so, having not fulfilled our duty, full of remorse and regret, we were reborn in a lesser realm.’

an9.19:2.1.pli: Aparāpi maṁ, bhikkhave, sambahulā devatā upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ:
an9.19:2.1.eng: Then several other deities came to me and said:

an9.19:2.2.pli: ‘upasaṅkamiṁsu no, bhante, pubbe manussabhūtānaṁ pabbajitā agārāni.
an9.19:2.2.eng: ‘Sir, formerly when we were human beings, renunciates came to our homes.

an9.19:2.3.pli: Te mayaṁ, bhante, paccuṭṭhimha abhivādimha, no ca tesaṁ āsanaṁ adamha.
an9.19:2.3.eng: We politely rose for them and bowed, but we didn’t offer a seat.

an9.19:2.4.pli: Tā mayaṁ, bhante, aparipuṇṇakammantā vippaṭisāriniyo paccānutāpiniyo hīnaṁ kāyaṁ upapannā’ti.
an9.19:2.4.eng: And so, having not fulfilled our duty, full of remorse and regret, we were reborn in a lesser realm.’

an9.19:3.1.pli: Aparāpi maṁ, bhikkhave, sambahulā devatā upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ:
an9.19:3.1.eng: Then several other deities came to me and said:

an9.19:3.2.pli: ‘upasaṅkamiṁsu no, bhante, pubbe manussabhūtānaṁ pabbajitā agārāni.
an9.19:3.2.eng: ‘Sir, formerly when we were human beings, renunciates came to our homes.

an9.19:3.3.pli: Te mayaṁ, bhante, paccuṭṭhimha abhivādimha āsanaṁ adamha, no ca kho yathāsatti yathābalaṁ saṁvibhajimha …pe…
an9.19:3.3.eng: We politely rose for them, bowed, and offered a seat, but we didn’t share as best we could. …’

an9.19:3.4.pli: yathāsatti yathābalaṁ saṁvibhajimha, no ca kho upanisīdimha dhammassavanāya …pe…
an9.19:3.4.eng: ‘… we didn’t sit nearby to listen to the teachings. …’

an9.19:3.5.pli: upanisīdimha dhammassavanāya, no ca kho ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇimha …pe…
an9.19:3.5.eng: ‘… we didn’t actively listen to the teachings. …’

an9.19:3.6.pli: ohitasotā ca dhammaṁ suṇimha, no ca kho sutvā dhammaṁ dhārayimha …pe…

an9.19:3.7.pli: sutvā ca dhammaṁ dhārayimha, no ca kho dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthaṁ upaparikkhimha …pe…
an9.19:3.7.eng: ‘… we didn’t examine the meaning of teachings we’d memorized. …’

an9.19:3.8.pli: dhātānañca dhammānaṁ atthaṁ upaparikkhimha, no ca kho atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammaṁ paṭipajjimha.
an9.19:3.8.eng: ‘… not having understood the meaning and the teaching, we didn’t practice in line with the teaching.

an9.19:3.9.pli: Tā mayaṁ, bhante, aparipuṇṇakammantā vippaṭisāriniyo paccānutāpiniyo hīnaṁ kāyaṁ upapannā’ti.
an9.19:3.9.eng: And so, having not fulfilled our duty, full of remorse and regret, we were reborn in a lesser realm.’

an9.19:4.1.pli: Aparāpi maṁ, bhikkhave, sambahulā devatā upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ:
an9.19:4.1.eng: Then several other deities came to me and said:

an9.19:4.2.pli: ‘upasaṅkamiṁsu no, bhante, pubbe manussabhūtānaṁ pabbajitā agārāni.
an9.19:4.2.eng: ‘Sir, formerly when we were human beings, renunciates came to our homes.

an9.19:4.3.pli: Te mayaṁ, bhante, paccuṭṭhimha abhivādimha, āsanaṁ adamha, yathāsatti yathābalaṁ saṁvibhajimha, upanisīdimha dhammassavanāya, ohitasotā ca dhammaṁ suṇimha, sutvā ca dhammaṁ dhārayimha, dhātānañca dhammānaṁ atthaṁ upaparikkhimha, atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammaṁ paṭipajjimha.
an9.19:4.3.eng: We politely rose, bowed, and offered them a seat. We shared as best we could. We sat nearby to listen to the teachings, lent an ear, memorized them, and examined their meaning. Understanding the teaching and the meaning we practiced in line with the teaching.

an9.19:4.4.pli: Tā mayaṁ, bhante, paripuṇṇakammantā avippaṭisāriniyo apaccānutāpiniyo paṇītaṁ kāyaṁ upapannā’ti.
an9.19:4.4.eng: And so, having fulfilled our duty, free of remorse and regret, we were reborn in a superior realm.’

an9.19:4.5.pli: Etāni, bhikkhave, rukkhamūlāni etāni suññāgārāni. Jhāyatha, bhikkhave, mā pamādattha, mā pacchā vippaṭisārino ahuvattha seyyathāpi tā purimikā devatā”ti.
an9.19:4.5.eng: Here, mendicants, are these roots of trees, and here are these empty huts. Practice absorption, mendicants! Don’t be negligent! Don’t regret it later, like those former deities.”

an9.2:1.1.pli: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
an9.2:1.1.eng: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:

an9.2:1.3.pli: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, bhikkhu nissayasampanno hotī”ti?
an9.2:1.3.eng: How is a mendicant who is supported defined?”

an9.2:1.4.pli: “Saddhañce, bhikkhu, bhikkhu nissāya akusalaṁ pajahati kusalaṁ bhāveti, pahīnamevassa taṁ akusalaṁ hoti.
an9.2:1.4.eng: “Mendicant, if a mendicant supported by faith gives up the unskillful and develops the skillful, the unskillful is actually given up by them.

an9.2:1.5.eng: If a mendicant supported by conscience …

an9.2:1.6.eng: If a mendicant supported by prudence …

an9.2:1.7.eng: If a mendicant supported by energy …

an9.2:1.8.pli: paññañce, bhikkhu, bhikkhu nissāya akusalaṁ pajahati kusalaṁ bhāveti, pahīnamevassa taṁ akusalaṁ hoti.
an9.2:1.8.eng: If a mendicant supported by wisdom gives up the unskillful and develops the skillful, the unskillful is actually given up by them.

an9.2:1.9.pli: Taṁ hissa bhikkhuno akusalaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti suppahīnaṁ, yaṁsa ariyāya paññāya disvā pahīnaṁ.
an9.2:1.9.eng: What’s been given up is completely given up when it has been given up by seeing with noble wisdom.

an9.2:2.1.pli: Tena ca pana, bhikkhu, bhikkhunā imesu pañcasu dhammesu patiṭṭhāya cattāro upanissāya vihātabbā.
an9.2:2.1.eng: But then, a mendicant grounded on these five things should rely on four things.

an9.2:2.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhu, bhikkhu saṅkhāyekaṁ paṭisevati, saṅkhāyekaṁ adhivāseti, saṅkhāyekaṁ parivajjeti, saṅkhāyekaṁ vinodeti.
an9.2:2.3.eng: After appraisal, a mendicant uses some things, endures some things, avoids some things, and gets rid of some things.

an9.2:2.4.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhu, bhikkhu nissayasampanno hotī”ti.
an9.2:2.4.eng: That’s how a mendicant is supported.”
